title: ECP Finding v1
description: Single structured finding emitted by a v2 specialist auditor or the ethics subagent. JSON is the source of truth; markdown audit-mirror, HTML report, and bulleted exports all render from this shape. Mechanically validatable. Authored 2026-04-27 brainstorm; see docs/brainstorms/2026-04-27-ecp-v2-architecture-brainstorm.md and docs/redesign-v2-proposal.md §21.4.3.

cluster.description: Which specialist cluster emitted this finding. The ethics subagent uses 'ethics'.

device.description: Which device the finding was emitted against. For scope='page' findings the synthesizer renders into both device documents; for scope='device' findings only the matching device document. Ethics findings (cluster='ethics') use device='page' to indicate evaluation against the union of devices — the cluster-emission level enforces the cluster='ethics' ↔ device='page' invariant.

local_id.description: 1-based index of this finding within its cluster file for this device. Forms F-{NN} references in Priority Path stories (e.g., 'pricing F-03').

verdict.description: Whether the page passes the audited rule. PASS findings describe what the page does well and may legitimately reference generic patterns; FAIL and PARTIAL findings require concrete evidence anchors.

title.description: Short identifier for the finding. Must name the specific element or sub-issue (not match the surface slug). Unique within the (cluster, device) pair. <=100 characters (relaxed from 60 after Phase B/C/D smoke testing showed real specialist titles run 70-90 chars when naming both the element and the sub-issue).

surface.description: The page surface this finding is about. Slug-style; Phase L makes this a closed enum: MUST be one of the cluster's surface_vocabulary, a baton.sections[].slug, or the literal 'other' (paired with surface_note). Validator: scripts/assembly/business_rules.py:_check_surface_in_vocabulary.

surface_note.description: Phase L: free-form note explaining the finding's surface choice. REQUIRED when surface='other' (signals the cluster vocabulary should grow to cover this concept). Omit otherwise. Validator: scripts/assembly/business_rules.py:_check_surface_in_vocabulary.

element.description: Reference to the specific captured element this finding targets. baton_index is the primary key — renderer hotspots resolve via dictionary lookup, never fuzzy CSS-selector matching. text_content and role are non-authoritative hints for human readability and synthesizer integration.

baton_index.description: Stable element index from baton.elements[]. Format 'e<int>' (e.g., 'e23'). Renderer renders the hotspot at this element's captured pixel coordinates.

oneOf.description: Reserved sentinel for findings that legitimately reference an element NOT in baton.elements[] — e.g., 'sticky CTA absence' findings where the missing element has no DOM presence to capture. Render with a section-level hotspot instead of an element-level one. Should be rare; the acquirer's element capture rules should cover most real elements.

text_content.description: Visible text or accessible name of the element (for synthesizer prose integration). Relaxed to 256 chars after Phase B/C/D testing showed specialists also use this field to record canonical URLs, meta-description quotes, and other identifying strings for 'absent' elements where there is no DOM text but identification context is useful.

role.description: Element role (button, link, heading, image, form, navigation, etc.) for synthesizer prose integration. When element.baton_index is a real e_index (not 'absent'), this MUST agree with the cited baton element's role under a small alias map (banner↔header, link↔a, image↔img). Coordinator-level lint enforces this in scripts/assembly/business_rules.py:_check_element_text_match.

tag.description: Optional HTML tag of the cited element (button, header, img, div, etc.). When provided AND element.baton_index is a real e_index, MUST match baton.elements[].tag exactly (case-insensitive). Lets coordinator lint catch hallucinated selectors like 'div.announcement-bar' when the cited e_index is actually a <header>. Specialists are encouraged to copy this verbatim from baton.elements[<index>].tag rather than infer it from the screenshot.

proposed_anchor.description: Placement hint for findings whose target element does not exist on the page (element.baton_index = 'absent'). Tells the renderer where the missing thing should appear if it existed. Discriminated union on `kind`: 'element' anchors near a real baton element, 'section' anchors inside a baton section, 'viewport' anchors at a page-global viewport position (sticky bars, above-fold persistence). Section-bound behaviors (e.g. trust badges entering the purchase zone) MUST use kind='section' with placement='section-bottom-overlay', NOT kind='viewport' with a section-bound trigger — that keeps kind='viewport' truly page-global. The `reason` field is operator-tooltip prose ONLY; the renderer MUST NEVER parse it for placement logic. Architectural fix B (2026-04-30): replaces brittle surface-string matching for absent findings.

kind.description: Discriminator for the placement variant. Drives the oneOf branching below.

element_baton_index.description: REQUIRED when kind='element'. Baton e_index of the real element to anchor relative to. Format 'e<int>'. FORBIDDEN for kind='section' or kind='viewport'.

section_index.description: REQUIRED when kind='section'. 0-based index into baton.sections[]. FORBIDDEN for kind='element' or kind='viewport'.

viewport_trigger.description: REQUIRED when kind='viewport'. Closed enum of page-global trigger conditions. 'after_primary_cta_offscreen': fires once the page's primary CTA scrolls past the viewport (sticky-CTA case). 'before_first_scroll': applies on initial page load, no scroll needed (above-fold persistence case). Section-bound triggers were intentionally dropped from v1 — use kind='section' for those. FORBIDDEN for kind='element' or kind='section'.

placement.description: Where the renderer pins the hotspot relative to the anchor target. Values are constrained per 'kind' by the oneOf below — element variants use the element-relative values, section uses 'section-bottom-overlay' (inside the section near its bottom edge) or 'after-section' (immediately after the section ends, before the next one begins), viewport uses 'viewport-bottom-sticky'. The schema rejects cross-variant placements via oneOf. NOTE on section placements: 'section-bottom-overlay' means INSIDE this section, near its bottom — use for in-section overlay annotations. 'after-section' means AFTER this section ends — use for content that visually belongs in the gap between this section and the next (e.g., 'trust strip below the featured collection').

viewport.description: Which device viewport this placement applies to. Behaviors that apply to BOTH devices must emit two findings (one per device specialist) — there is no 'both' value in v1.

reason.description: Free-form prose for the operator-facing hotspot tooltip explaining why this placement was chosen. EXCLUSIVELY for human display. The renderer MUST NOT parse this field for placement logic — encode anything load-bearing in the structured fields above.

oneOf.title: kind=element variant
oneOf.description: Pin near a real baton element. Use when the missing thing has a natural anchor element on the page (e.g. MSRP missing above the live price element).

oneOf.title: kind=section variant
oneOf.description: Pin in or after a baton section. Use 'section-bottom-overlay' when the missing thing belongs INSIDE a section near its bottom (e.g. payment method badges absent from the purchase-zone section). Use 'after-section' when the missing thing belongs in the gap AFTER a section ends (e.g. trust strip below the featured-collection grid). The two were conflated in earlier schema versions; specialists must now distinguish them.

oneOf.title: kind=viewport variant
oneOf.description: Pin at a page-global viewport position. Use ONLY when the missing thing is a TRULY page-global viewport behavior (sticky bars, above-fold persistence). Section-bound behaviors use kind='section' instead.

severity.description: Impact tier of the finding. CRITICAL is reserved for ethics BLOCK findings. HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW correspond to the v1 PRIORITY field.

scope.description: Whether the finding applies to the entire page (both devices) or only this specific device's viewport. page-scope findings are deduplicated cross-device and rendered with synchronized prose in both device audit documents; device-scope findings appear only in their own device's document.

effort.description: Decomposed effort estimate for fixing this finding. Used by the synthesizer's Quick Wins Priority Path mode (filters change_type IN {copy, css, html-attr} AND change_scope = single-file or component) without an additional LLM call. Phase 7 (2026-05-18) adds 'not_applicable' for CLEAR ethics findings — those record 'we checked and it's fine' with no change to ship; the enforced contract below (in allOf) requires CLEAR ethics findings to use not_applicable in both fields and rejects not_applicable for any other finding.

change_type.description: Class of code/content change required. Use 'not_applicable' ONLY for CLEAR ethics findings (no change to ship).

change_scope.description: Footprint of the change in the codebase. Use 'not_applicable' ONLY for CLEAR ethics findings (no change to ship).

confidence.description: Specialist's self-rated confidence in the finding, 0.0-1.0. Used by the synthesizer's Severity Priority Path mode (sort by severity x evidence_tier x confidence) without an additional LLM call. Default 0.8 if absent.

evidence_anchors.description: Concrete tie-points to the page under audit. At least one anchor required for FAIL/PARTIAL findings (the specialist must show its work) — enforced via the conditional rule in allOf below, NOT via top-level minItems (PASS findings may omit). DOM-only findings (alt text, h1 absence, schema) anchor to dom; visual findings (above-fold position, contrast) MUST include at least one visual anchor.

reference.description: For type=dom: a baton element index ('e23') or a unique DOM path. For type=visual: a screenshot file reference ('section-3.jpg' or 'section-3-mobile.jpg').

scroll_y.description: For visual anchors: the scrollY position where the element appears, in pixels. Required for findings that make above-fold/below-fold/sticky claims.

viewport.description: For visual anchors: which device viewport the screenshot is from. Defaults to the finding's device.

context.description: Optional human-readable context describing what this anchor evidences. Synthesizer may quote. Relaxed to 800 chars after Phase B/C/D smoke testing (real anchor contexts run up to ~660 chars when describing what's visible in the screenshot or DOM slice with enough specificity to verify against the page).

reference_citations.description: Citations to ECP reference files. At least one required for FAIL/PARTIAL findings. PASS findings exempt. The dedup engine uses the highest-tier citation as the kept finding's anchor.

source.description: Reference filename relative to references/ directory. Must end in .md.

line.description: Optional line number in the reference file the finding cites. When present, allows the synthesizer to quote the specific source line.

section.description: Optional section heading or anchor in the reference file. Mutually exclusive with line; either may be used. Relaxed to 160 chars after Phase B/C/D testing showed real reference section headings run 80-100+ chars (e.g., 'Finding 1: Customer Video and Photo UGC Can Increase Conversion 80% Over Professional-Only Content').

tier.description: Strength of evidence per references/evidence-tiers.md. Gold = controlled studies / authoritative bodies; Silver = case studies / industry data; Bronze = expert pattern recognition.

observation.description: What the specialist observed, in client-facing prose. Plain language; no internal jargon (no 'baton', 'DOM', 'cluster', 'context'); no first-person scan narration ('I searched...', 'A thorough search of...'); no token counts or context-management commentary. The synthesizer extends the senior-strategist humanizer pass over this field in v2. Top-level minLength is 1 (PASS findings may be terse — 'Hero CTA is the highest-contrast element on the page'). The conditional rule in allOf below bumps minLength to 20 for FAIL/PARTIAL findings, where the specialist must show its work.

recommendation.description: What the specialist recommends doing about it. Lead with the gate ('If X, then Y'); avoid 'Do X. But do not Z.' construction (negation-after-affirmation produces muddled reasoning per §18.2.5). The synthesizer's senior-strategist humanizer pass extends to this field in v2 (closes §18.2.5 — v1 only humanized observation). Top-level minLength is 1 (PASS findings may be terse or even reduce to a 'keep doing this' affirmation). The conditional rule in allOf below bumps minLength to 20 for FAIL/PARTIAL findings.

why_this_matters.description: Stakes statement: what happens to conversion / trust / accessibility / legal exposure if this finding goes unaddressed. One to two sentences typical; longer when citing research effect sizes, dollar figures, or regulatory penalty schedules. Relaxed to 1000 chars after Phase B/C/D testing.

evidence_tier.description: Highest tier across reference_citations[]. Computed at emission time, not derived. Used by dedup tie-breaker (Gold > Silver > Bronze) and Priority Path Severity-mode sort.

ethics_state.description: Set ONLY by the ethics subagent. cluster='ethics' findings carry this field; cluster auditor findings do not (cluster auditors run zero ethics rules in v2). BLOCK = audit fails until resolved; ADJACENT = warning surfaced to operator; CLEAR = ethics-cleared but worth noting.

source_url.description: When ethics_state is BLOCK or ADJACENT, the SOURCE_URL from ethics-gate.md's Source Registry that justifies the finding. Required for ethics findings; absent for cluster auditor findings.

merged_from.description: When dedup merges multiple specialist findings into one, the kept finding records the source finding identifiers here. Format: ['<cluster> F-<NN>', ...]. Empty for findings that were never merged.

intentional_outside_registry.description: Phase 4b (2026-05-18) escape hatch: when true, this finding's element.baton_index is deliberately NOT one of the candidates in anchor-candidates-{device}.json. Used when the candidate ranker missed an element the specialist needs to anchor against. Requires intentional_outside_registry_reason to be a non-empty string. Default false. See contracts/specialist-prompt-v2.md 'Anchor candidates' section.

intentional_outside_registry_reason.description: Free-form prose explaining WHY this finding anchors against an element outside the candidate registry. Required when intentional_outside_registry=true. Logged for ranker tuning — every opt-out signals a class the classifier should be taught to recognize.

visual_evidence.description: Phase 2 visual evidence taxonomy (2026-05-18). Separates FINDING IDENTITY from HOW THE REPORT REPRESENTS IT VISUALLY. The pre-Phase-2 renderer inferred visual treatment from baton_index alone, which caused absent findings (no sticky CTA, no reviews) to render as generic circular proxy markers on the wrong region of the page. visual_evidence makes the visual contract explicit per finding. Optional field — legacy emissions without it are accepted; scripts/report/visual_evidence.derive_visual_evidence() back-fills the type by inspecting match_method + proposed_anchor + baton_index. Producers SHOULD populate this field explicitly; consumers MUST tolerate its absence. See docs/ecp/2026-05-18-report-accuracy-and-hotspot-remediation-plan.md Phase 2.

type.description: How the visual report should represent this finding. exact_element: tight selector matching a specific DOM element the finding is about (solid rect). proxy_element: a real baton element near the finding's subject but not exactly it (dashed rect). generated_expected_zone: a ghost/dashed overlay showing where the missing UI SHOULD appear (use for 'no sticky CTA', 'no reviews block', 'no payment badges'). section_absence: a section-level marker for absences that don't have a single anchor point (pill). page_level: a banner spanning the page header for engagement-wide signals (e.g., 'DOM was edited during capture').

confidence.description: How sure the producer is that the visual representation is correct. high: anchor element is verbatim-cited by name and the rect is tight (Codex-style direct selector). medium: anchor is a real baton element near the finding's subject but the rect may include extra siblings (proxy). low: section or viewport anchor with implicit placement (generated expected zone). needs_review: producer punted on placement, operator should pick the exact marker location in editor.html before shipping. Phase 3 quality gates reject Priority Path and ethics findings with needs_review.

observed_anchor.description: When type is exact_element or proxy_element, the baton element this finding visually anchors to. Either baton_index (direct eN reference) OR candidate_id (Phase 4a anchor candidate registry reference; resolved through anchor-candidates-{device}.json's candidate_to_e_index map) — at least one must be present. selector_hint and text_quote are for human-readable hover tooltips and operator review; the renderer uses baton_index (or the resolved candidate_id → baton_index lookup) for the actual lookup.

candidate_id.description: Phase 4a: stable candidate ID from anchor-candidates-{device}.json's candidates_by_role buckets (e.g., 'primary-cta-1', 'price-block-1', 'trust-strip-2'). The lead's post-validation layer resolves this through candidate_to_e_index map and substitutes the canonical baton_index. Specialists may use candidate_id INSTEAD OF baton_index in Phase 4a (side-channel); Phase 4b makes it mandatory.

expected_overlay.description: When type is generated_expected_zone, the template the renderer should draw to represent the missing UI. template_id is the registered overlay name (sticky-cta, reviews-block, payment-badges, trust-strip, video-tile, msrp-anchor, etc. — extensible). anchor_relative_to is the baton element or section the overlay should be positioned against. placement names the relative position (above, below, inside-bottom, sticky-viewport-bottom, etc.).

reason.description: Free-form prose explaining the visual evidence choice. Operator-facing tooltip only — the renderer MUST NOT parse this for placement logic. Use it to explain WHY the producer chose this type (e.g., 'No real element exists for missing sticky CTA; ghost overlay at viewport bottom').

allOf.description: FAIL and PARTIAL findings must cite at least one reference.
FAIL and PARTIAL findings must include at least one evidence anchor (PASS findings may omit).

FAIL and PARTIAL findings must have substantial observation and recommendation prose (>=20 chars each); the specialist must show its work. PASS findings may be terse — they're recording 'this works' without needing to justify a fix. SpecFlow gap #11.
Ethics findings (cluster='ethics') with BLOCK or ADJACENT ethics_state require a source_url from the ethics-gate Source Registry.
Non-ethics findings must NOT carry ethics_state. Ethics is a property of the ethics subagent's output only — cluster auditors do not emit ethics findings in v2.
evidence_tier promotion: any Gold citation forces evidence_tier=Gold.
evidence_tier promotion: any Silver citation (and no Gold) forces evidence_tier=Silver. Combined with the Gold rule above, this enforces evidence_tier == max(citation tiers).

Findings whose element.baton_index is 'absent' MUST emit proposed_anchor. Without it the renderer falls back to a generic top-of-page banner indicator that visually flattens every absent finding to the same point on the page. Architectural fix B (2026-04-30).
Visual position findings (above-fold, below-fold, sticky claims) must include at least one visual evidence_anchor with scroll_y. Closes §18.2.3 vehicle-selector-position-error class.

Phase 4b (2026-05-18) — when intentional_outside_registry is true, the reason field must be a non-empty string. This pins the contract that every opt-out from the anchor candidate registry carries a human-readable justification for ranker tuning.

Phase 7 (2026-05-18) — CLEAR ethics findings MUST use change_type/change_scope = not_applicable. These findings record 'we checked the surface and it's fine' with no change to ship; pretending a copy/single-file change exists (the pre-Phase-7 workaround the awdmods 2026-05-18 run had to inline-patch when the specialist emitted change_type=none) misrepresents the data. Closes Codex Q7 from the original 13-question review.

Phase 7 (2026-05-18) — non-CLEAR findings (every cluster except ethics, plus ethics BLOCK/ADJACENT) MUST NOT use not_applicable for change_type. Those findings DO have a change to ship; not_applicable is reserved for the 'we checked and it's fine' shape. This rule + the CLEAR-requires-not_applicable rule together pin the contract symmetrically.

examples.title: ATC Button Color Blends Into Page

evidence_anchors.context: Add to Cart button uses #6B6B6B against white card; matches three other mid-grey UI elements above the fold.

examples.observation: The Add to Cart button uses a mid-grey fill that matches three other interactive elements within the same viewport. Without a distinct primary-action color, the button has no visual claim to being the most important action on the page; visitors scanning for the buy decision have to read every grey element to find it.
examples.recommendation: If the brand has a primary accent color (the SlingMods red), apply it as the Add to Cart button's background and reserve that color for purchase actions only. Do not introduce a new color the brand doesn't already own — pull the accent from the logo or an existing high-priority surface so the action color reads as intentional.
examples.why_this_matters: On mobile product pages, the Add to Cart button is the only conversion action that matters; if it doesn't visually own the page, every conversion costs more attention than it should.

examples.title: Privacy Policy Links to Non-Canonical Staging Domain

evidence_anchors.context: href points to e1520g-k3.myshopify.com, the store's pre-launch staging domain, not the canonical awdmods.com policy.

examples.observation: The footer privacy policy link points at the store's Shopify staging domain instead of a canonical first-party URL on the storefront. Visitors clicking through expect the policy to be hosted by the store they're buying from; instead they land on an infrastructure domain the store does not visibly control, which weakens the regulatory chain of disclosure.
examples.recommendation: Replace the staging-domain href with a first-party policy URL on the canonical store domain. If the policy is currently only hosted on the staging domain, mirror the content to a /policies/privacy-policy route on the production domain and update all storefront links to that path.
examples.why_this_matters: EU and California disclosure regimes assume the policy URL is operated by the merchant. A staging-domain redirect creates ambiguity about who controls the policy text, which is exactly the disclosure failure mode the EAA and CCPA require merchants to avoid.
